rationale: The citing paper explicitly states that to handle endogeneity in cross-selling solicitations, it follows the approach of Manchanda, Rossi, and Chintagunta (2004). This is used in the model specification and estimation section, where solicitation is modeled as a function of household response parameters rather than treated as fully exogenous. Because the cited work informs a concrete identification/modeling choice in the empirical framework, the citation plays a meaningful methodological role beyond background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Manchanda et al. (2004)'s treatment of nonrandom marketing actions to its cross-selling setting by modeling solicitation assignment endogeneity within its hierarchical Bayesian estimation framework.
